children.text: developing or implementing leadership training programs designed to prepare and support principals or other school leaders in high-need schools, including through new or alternative pathways or school leader residency programs;
developing or implementing programs or activities for recruiting, selecting, and developing aspiring or current principals or other school leaders to serve in high-need schools;

developing or implementing programs for recruiting, developing, and placing school leaders to improve schools implementing comprehensive support and improvement activities and targeted support and improvement activities under , including through cohort-based activities that build effective instructional and school leadership teams and develop a school culture, design, instructional program, and professional development program focused on improving student learning;section 6311(d) of this title
providing continuous professional development for principals or other school leaders in high-need schools;

developing and disseminating information on best practices and strategies for effective school leadership in high-need schools, such as training and supporting principals to identify, develop, and maintain school leadership teams using various leadership models; and
other evidence-based programs or activities described in or focused on principals or other school leaders in high-need schools.section 6611(c)(4) of this titlesection 6613(b)(3) of this title

children.heading: Program periods and diversity of projects
children.label: b Program periods and diversity of projects

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A grant awarded by the Secretary to an eligible entity under this section shall be for a period of not more than 5 years.

children.heading: Renewal
children.label: 2 Renewal
children.text: The Secretary may renew a grant awarded under this section for 1 additional 2-year period.

children.heading: Diversity of projects
children.label: 3 Diversity of projects
children.text: In awarding grants under this section, the Secretary shall ensure that, to the extent practicable, grants are distributed among eligible entities that will serve geographically diverse areas, including urban, suburban, and rural areas.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 4 Limitation
children.text: The Secretary shall not award more than 1 grant under this section to an eligible entity during a grant competition.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An eligible entity that receives a grant under this section shall provide, from non-Federal sources, not less than 25 percent of the funds for the total cost for each year of activities carried out under this section.

children.heading: Acceptable contributions
children.label: 2 Acceptable contributions
children.text: An eligible entity that receives a grant under this section may meet the requirement of paragraph (1) by providing contributions in cash or in kind, fairly evaluated, including plant, equipment, and services.

children.heading: Waivers
children.label: 3 Waivers
children.text: The Secretary may waive or modify the requirement of paragraph (1) in cases of demonstrated financial hardship.

children.heading: Applications
children.label: d Applications
children.text: An eligible entity that desires a grant under this section shall submit to the Secretary an application at such time, and in such manner, as the Secretary may require.

children.label: A
children.text: have improved school-level student outcomes;

children.label: B
children.text: have become principals in high-need schools; and

children.label: C
children.text: remain principals in high-need schools for multiple years; and
who will implement evidence-based activities, defined for the purpose of this paragraph as activities meeting the requirements of .section 7801(21)(A)(i) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a local educational agency, including an educational service agency, that serves a high-need school or a consortium of such agencies;

children.label: B
children.text: a State educational agency or a consortium of such agencies;

children.label: C
children.text: a State educational agency in partnership with 1 or more local educational agencies, or educational service agencies, that serve a high-need school;

children.label: D
children.text: the Bureau of Indian Education; or

children.label: E
children.text: an entity described in subparagraph (A), (B), (C), or (D) in partnership with 1 or more nonprofit organizations or institutions of higher education.

children.label: A
children.text: an elementary school in which not less than 50 percent of the enrolled students are from families with incomes below the poverty line; or

children.label: B
children.text: a secondary school in which not less than 40 percent of the enrolled students are from families with incomes below the poverty line.
